The user is asking for a short, polite two-paragraph email to opposing counsel proposing to extend a contract-review deadline by one week. They explicitly said:
- Generic wording is fine

- They just need the email text

This is a simple drafting task. The user has been clear: no need to reference the matter's documents, no need to search for any specific contract details. They just want generic email text.

But the user explicitly said generic wording is fine and not to reference any specific agreement. So I should respect the user's explicit instruction and not search the documents for specifics that they don't want included.

The task is simple — just draft a two-paragraph email. I don't need to use any tools. I should just write the email directly. This is a trivial task that doesn't need write_todos or subagents.

Let me draft a concise, professional, polite two-paragraph email:

Paragraph 1: Greeting, acknowledge the current deadline, and the reason for needing more time (without specifics, just a general need for additional review time).

Paragraph 2: Propose the one-week extension, suggest a new date, express appreciation, and close professionally.

Let me write it:

Subject: Request for Brief Extension on Contract Review Deadline

Dear [Counsel],

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to request a brief extension of our current contract-review deadline by one week. Given the complexity of the issues involved and some competing internal priorities on our end, an additional seven days would allow our team to conduct a more thorough review and provide more substantive comments.

If this works for you and your client, we would propose moving the deadline to [new date], which would preserve the momentum of our discussions while ensuring the quality of our review. Please let me know at your convenience if this accommodation is acceptable, or if you would prefer to discuss alternative arrangements. Thank you for your understanding, and I look forward to your response.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

That looks good. Let me refine it a bit to make it more concise and polished.
</think>

Subject: Request for Brief Extension on Contract Review Deadline

Dear [Counsel],

I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to request a brief extension of our current contract-review deadline by one week. Due to a few scheduling constraints on our end, an additional seven days would allow our team to complete a more thorough review and provide more substantive comments.

If that works for you and your client, we would propose moving the deadline to [new date]. Please let me know at your convenience if this is acceptable, or if you would prefer to discuss alternative arrangements. Thank you for your consideration, and I look forward to your response.

Best regards,
[Your Name]
